title: Accepting Bitcoin for Digital Product Sales: Which Option Works Regardless of Where You Are
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: You made the product. The platform blocked you. Here is how to sell it anyway — to anyone, anywhere, with no gatekeeper in the way.

content.text: Having a great digital product to sell is only half the battle, as many creators know all too well, especially those from restricted countries where platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are not available, leaving them with the daunting task of finding alternative payment solutions, and more importantly, a reliable way to deliver their products to customers, a challenge that can be daunting and frustrating, especially when you have a product that you know people want to buy, but you are stuck on how to get paid and deliver it to them, a problem that Unchained Commerce has been helping creators solve for a long time now
What You Are Actually Comparing

When considering payment options for your digital products, it is essential to understand what you are comparing, and in this case, it is the traditional platforms that have failed many creators due to their restrictive policies and geographical limitations, versus crypto-native infrastructure that offers a more inclusive and flexible solution, allowing creators to sell their products and get paid in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors, and with the added benefit of automated delivery and payment verification
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip have failed many creators, especially those from restricted countries, due to their strict KYC requirements, geographical limitations, and high fees, making it difficult for creators to sell their products and get paid, and even when they do, the payment process is often slow and cumbersome, with a high risk of account suspension or termination, a risk that many creators are not willing to take, and one that can be avoided with crypto-native infrastructure
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, offers a more inclusive and flexible solution, allowing creators to sell their products and get paid in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors, and with the added benefit of automated delivery and payment verification, making it a more reliable and efficient solution for creators, and one that can be easily integrated into their existing sales funnel, with products like Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, making it easy to manage and track sales, and provide a better customer experience
The biggest obstacle for many creators is not the quality of their product, but rather the ability to get paid and deliver it to their customers, a problem that crypto-native infrastructure can solve
The Real Cost Difference

While traditional platforms may seem like a convenient option, the real cost difference lies in the fees and the risk of account suspension or termination, which can be devastating for creators who rely on these platforms for their livelihood, and with crypto-native infrastructure, the cost is a one-time payment, with no recurring fees or subscriptions, and with the added benefit of lifetime access, making it a more cost-effective solution in the long run, and one that can provide creators with more control over their sales and revenue

So, which one to use and why, the answer is simple, if you are a creator who wants to sell digital products and get paid in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors, and with the added benefit of automated delivery and payment verification, then crypto-native infrastructure is the way to go, and with products like Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, you can easily integrate it into your existing sales funnel, and start selling and getting paid right away

For creators who want to sell ebooks, courses, templates, fonts, presets, beats, stock photos, plugins, or software, and are looking for a way to do so without the restrictions of traditional platforms, crypto-native infrastructure is the way to go, and with the ability to sell and get paid in a variety of cryptocurrencies, and with the added benefit of automated delivery and payment verification, it is a solution that can provide creators with more control over their sales and revenue, and one that can help them reach a wider audience
